programmes.show_description: Political magazine show hosted by Robert Peston, featuring major interviews with MPs, topical guests and cultural figures. Including updates from social media taking in perspectives from viewers, experts and key players throughout the programme

programmes.show_name: Spring Clean: Keep, Chuck or Cash In? Tonight
programmes.show_description: Nearly half of the UK is living with stress-inducing clutter, but are the unwanted items lying around homes trash or treasure? The team investigates why many find it difficult to have a clear out, why having too much clutter can be bad for the health, and what viewers could upcycle or exchange for cash

Ainsley Harriott tries his hand at straw-weaving before learning how to cook grouper Nassau-style, taking in a lesson in 'conch-servation' down at the Fish Fry, and following that up with rum punch chicken wings in his kitchen on the beach
Daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection

programmes.show_name: Gary Barlow's Wine Tour: South Africa
programmes.show_description: The Take That veteran heads into Johannesburg to meet soul sensation and friend Mica Paris. The pair spend time at Sanctuary Mandela, taking in the history of the great man, hearing live music, and sampling fantastic food, before heading to Soweto for a community braai, an event featuring great wine and entertainment

Stephen Mulhern hosts another charity edition of the game show, with Chris Bisson, Ruth Madeley and Ardal O'Hanlon trying to say what they see as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen good causes

The fast-paced game of words, hosted by Adil Ray, where three pairs of contestants face Lingo grids, fiendish puzzlewords, and a tense head-to-head round as they battle to reach the final and a chance to win a 15,000-pound jackpot. Stephen Mulhern hosts the revival of the game show as a contestant tries to open the 22 red boxes in the right order, taking on the infamous Banker for the chance to win a life-changing cash prize

Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programmes.show_name: Supermarket Prices: Does Loyalty Pay? - Tonight
programmes.show_description: Food prices have surged in recent years and are now almost 40 per cent higher than at the end of 2020. So what can we do to cut the cost of our weekly shop, and do loyalty schemes and in-store discounts really deliver? Kate Quilton reports

Jimmy Doherty and Shivi Ramoutar are joined in the kitchen by actor and comedian Kevin Bishop, who enjoys black pudding wontons for breakfast and a sweet spicy treat of a pineapple and chilli ccrème brûlée. Chantal Dawson hast tips on how to save ailing houseplants, Shivi learns how to make a natural woodland fire torch with bushcraft and survival expert Mark Beckham, and Jimmy gets up close and personal with some crocodiles and alligators.

programmes.show_name: Devil in Disguise: John Wayne Gacy
programmes.show_description: Kunkle and Sam are knee-deep in Gacy's trial as Tovar volunteers to drive Gacy to and from court, while attempting to compel him to come clean about how many people he killed, the disturbing tale of how Gacy befriended and groomed David Cram is revealed
